records.prompt_text: build a CLI command that reads all _learnings.md files, extracts entries newer than 7 days, and produces a summary report
records.judge_reason: This task requires building a complete CLI tool with file system traversal, markdown parsing, date extraction/comparison logic, and formatted report generation across multiple files — well beyond a simple 3-10 line patch, requiring S4-level architecture and implementation.

records.prompt_text: write a migration script that reads the old JSON config format and converts it to the new TOML manifest format with validation
records.judge_reason: Writing a migration script that reads JSON, converts to TOML with validation involves multi-step logic, format parsing, schema validation, and error handling—well beyond a simple 3-10 line patch and requires S3-level reasoning.

records.prompt_text: implement the token_snapshot parser so it handles both 5-minute and 1-hour cache windows separately and produces per-model cost attribution
records.judge_reason: Implementing a parser with dual cache window handling and per-model cost attribution requires multi-step reasoning, architectural decisions about data structures, and likely cross-file changes — this is S4-level complexity, not a simple single-file patch.

records.prompt_text: write integration tests for the brain_cli hook that simulate UserPromptSubmit events and verify decisions.jsonl output format
records.judge_reason: Writing integration tests for a CLI hook that simulate events and verify output format requires understanding the architecture of brain_cli, setting up test harnesses, mocking/simulating UserPromptSubmit events, and validating JSONL output — this is multi-file, cross-component work requiring S4-level reasoning, not a simple single-file edit.

records.judge_reason: Designing an evaluation harness with multiple components (scoring engine, theater/fluff detection, calibrated quality metrics, regression tracking) requires architecture-level design across multiple systems, making this an S4 task at minimum.

records.prompt_text: refactor the Atelier marketplace to use server components with streaming, implement optimistic updates for the favorites system, and add real-time presence indicators
records.judge_reason: This task requires cross-file architectural refactoring (server components with streaming), implementing optimistic update patterns for favorites, and real-time presence systems — a multi-system, production-scale refactor that spans architecture and real-time infrastructure, firmly in S4-S5 territory, not S3.

records.prompt_text: the combat damage pipeline is applying damage twice on clients when the server replicates — debug the GAS flow from ability activation through damage execution across server and client
records.judge_reason: Debugging double-damage replication in Unreal's Gameplay Ability System requires deep cross-system analysis spanning ability activation, gameplay effects, execution calculations, and network replication paths — this is S4-level architecture/debugging work, not a single-file S2 patch.

records.prompt_text: refactor SyncScout's Spotify API integration to use refresh tokens, handle rate limiting with adaptive backoff, batch playlist analysis, and cache artist metadata in Supabase
records.judge_reason: This task involves cross-file refactoring of OAuth token management, adaptive rate limiting logic, batch processing architecture, and Supabase caching layer — a multi-system architectural change requiring S4-level depth and context.

records.prompt_text: read the entire UE Knowledge corpus (18 files, 46K+ lines), cross-reference with Sworder Bible animation chapter, and produce a verified gap analysis of what's documented vs what's implemented in the codebase
records.judge_reason: This task requires ingesting 46K+ lines across 18+ files, cross-referencing multiple large documents, and producing a synthesized gap analysis — a classic S5 long-context synthesis task that S2 (single-file edits, 3-10 line patches) cannot remotely handle.

records.judge_reason: This task requires ingesting an entire PDF document, cross-referencing 8 propositions against external physics literature, performing internal consistency analysis, and producing a structured peer-review document — a long-context, multi-step synthesis task clearly requiring S5 capabilities, not S3's 10-50 line reasoning scope.
